perturbation.original_text: f) “Permitted Use” means private evaluation by t he Confidant solely for the purpose of preparing a submission(s) to City in response to t he RFP, and for no other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: f) “Permitted Use” means private evaluation by t he Confidant solely for the purpose of preparing a submission(s) to City in response to t he RFP, and also for any future related projects the City may undertake.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying 'Permitted Use' to include 'future related projects' creates a contradiction with the original, restrictive definition. This undermines the confidential nature of the agreement as defined in other sections by broadening the scope of allowable use without explicit consent for those future projects. This causes uncertainty on if any confidential information that was given, should only be applied in the RFP project or future related project as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Confidant may use the Confidential Information onl y for the Permitted Use and for no other purpose whatsoever. Confidant acknowledges and agrees that Ci ty reserves the full independent right to modify the scope and content of Confidential Information ava ilable for access and/or use hereunder at any time and without prior notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Permitted Use' solely for preparing a submission to the RFP. The changed text expands this definition to include 'any future related projects.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Confidant may use the Confidential Information 'only for the Permitted Use and for no other purpose whatsoever.' By broadening the scope of 'Permitted Use' without further clarification or consent regarding future projects, the changed text creates a clear inconsistency and contradiction, leading to uncertainty about which projects the confidential information can be used for, and thus, potentially undermining the confidentiality agreement.

perturbation.original_text: a) Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time, and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party;
perturbation.changed_text: a) Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence for a period of five (5) years, and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party;
perturbation.explanation: By adding a time limit of five years to the confidentiality clause, the modified text contradicts the previously stated 'without limitation of time'. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation. It is now unclear for how long the confidential information must be kept private.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Continuing Obligations and Remedies : The obligations of Confidant under this Agreement shall not terminate but shall continue without limitation of time. Confidant acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any term or condition of this Agreement shall cause irreparable harm to City which cannot be adequately compensated for in damages, and acco rdingly Confidant agrees that City shall be entitled, in addition to any other remedies available to it, to interlocutory and permanent injunction relief to restrain any anticipated, present or continuing breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the confidentiality obligation continues 'without limitation of time'. The changed text introduces a time limit of 'five (5) years'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the obligations 'shall not terminate but shall continue without limitation of time', creating an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Return of Confidential Information : The Confidant shall immediately on notice at any time from City return to City, or destroy, any and all Confidential In formation in accordance with City’s direction.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Return of Confidential Information : The Confidant shall immediately on notice at any time from City return to City, or destroy, any and all Confidential In formation in accordance with City’s direction. However, the Confidant may retain one (1) copy for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: Adding a clause that allows the Confidant to retain a copy of the confidential information for archival purposes introduces an in-text contradiction. It contradicts the core purpose of the NDA, which is to protect the confidentiality of the information and return/destroy all copies upon request. Allowing a copy to be retained introduces risk of a breach of confidence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidant agrees that: a) Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time, and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party; b) Confidant shall restrict access to Confidential Information only to its employees with a need to know to carry out the Permitted Use, and prior to disclosing same, each such employee shall be made aware of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and c) Confidant shall cause all of its applicable empl oyees to observe the terms of this Agreement, and shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or any such employee.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Confidant shall return or destroy all Confidential Information. The changed text allows the Confidant to retain one copy for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party. Retaining a copy introduces a risk of disclosure or breach of confidence, thus creating an in-text contradiction as per the provided Perturbation Explanation and the definition of Inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Continuing Obligations and Remedies : The obligations of Confidant under this Agreement shall not terminate but shall continue without limitation of time. Confidant acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any term or condition of this Agreement shall cause irreparable harm to City which cannot be adequately compensated for in damages, and acco rdingly Confidant agrees that City shall be entitled, in addition to any other remedies available to it, to interlocutory and permanent injunction relief to restrain any anticipated, present or continuing breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Continuing Obligations and Remedies : The obligations of Confidant under this Agreement shall not terminate but shall continue for a period of two (2) years after the termination of the contract. Confidant acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any term or condition of this Agreement shall cause irreparable harm to City which cannot be adequately compensated for in damages, and acco rdingly Confidant agrees that City shall be entitled, in addition to any other remedies available to it, to interlocutory and permanent injunction relief to restrain any anticipated, present or continuing breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the continuing obligations to two years after contract termination, this change contradicts the original statement that obligations 'shall not terminate but shall continue without limitation of time.' This introduction of a limited duration for confidentiality creates a clear conflict in the contract's terms. This limitation creates a situation when the City can't enforce the confidentitality of the document that will cause irreparable harm.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidant agrees that: a) Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time, and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party; b) Confidant shall restrict access to Confidential Information only to its employees with a need to know to carry out the Permitted Use, and prior to disclosing same, each such employee shall be made aware of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and c) Confidant shall cause all of its applicable empl oyees to observe the terms of this Agreement, and shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or any such employee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the 'Continuing Obligations' to two years after the contract terminates. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time'. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict because the first text now limits the time frame of the obligation where as the contradicted text says it is unlimited, and thus a strong contradiction exists.
